memoryArtifacts.headline:
Elizabeth Hammond Livermore
(1 April 1574 – June 1632)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Daughter of Thomas Hammond and Rose Trippe Hammond of Lavenham, Co. Suffolk.

Children: Martha Livermore, Daniel Livermore, John Livermore, and Sarah Livermore.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Hammond於1574年4月1日之前出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可拉文哈姆時，她的父親Thomas Hammond28歲，母親Rose Trippe21歲。她於1599年與George Kelsey在英國艾可傑爾姆斯福德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1631年11月25日在英國素福爾可，埋葬在英國素福爾可蕾托 瑟洛福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Hammond于1574年4月1日之前出生在英国英格兰素福爾可拉文哈姆时，她的父亲Thomas Hammond28岁，母亲Rose Trippe21岁。她于1599年与George Kelsey在英国艾可傑爾姆斯福德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1631年11月25日在英国素福爾可，埋葬在英国素福爾可蕾托 瑟洛福。

nameMeanings.name: Hammond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English, Old French personal name Ha(i)mon, the oblique case form of the ancient Germanic Ha(i)mo, a short form of various compound names beginning with haim ‘home’. It frequently developed excrescent -d, giving Hamond, Haimund, and Hawmond. Alternatively, the name could derive from the Middle English personal name Hamund (Old Norse Hámundr, composed of the elements hár ‘high’ + mund ‘protection’), which may have been used in Normandy and in 12th-century eastern England, but the former explanation is more likely. The surname was sometimes confused with Almond and Ammon .

English: in the Bradford area of Yorkshire, the name is a shortened form of Ormondroyd, formerly Hamondesrode, from a lost place in Birstall (Yorkshire), named with the Middle English (Old French) personal name Hamon (1 above) + Middle English roid, a southern Yorkshire pronunciation of Old English rod ‘clearing’.
Irish: generally an importation from England, but occasionally an adopted name for Mac Ámoinn, see McCammon .

variants.name: Hammonds
meanings: English (Shropshire and Staffordshire): variant of Hammond , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Almond
meanings: English:
from the Middle English personal name Almund, in which Old English Æthelmund ‘noble protector’ and Ealhmund ‘temple protector’ have coalesced.
variant of   Allman   and   Allmand   both meaning ‘German’, assimilated by folk etymology to the vocabulary word denoting the tree.

variants.name: Hammett
meanings: English (southwestern England, especialy Devon): from the Middle English personal name Hamet, a pet form of ancient Germanic Hamo (see Hammond 1).

variants.name: Hamon
meanings: Breton, French, and English (Channel Islands): from the Breton, Old French, Middle English personal name Hamon, the oblique case form of the ancient Germanic Ha(i)mo, a short form of various compound names beginning with haim ‘home’. See also Hammond .

variants.name: Hamlin
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, from the Norman French and Middle English personal name Hamelin, a double diminutive of Hamo (see Hammond ).
French: variant of   Hamelin   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Americanized form of Finnish Hämäläinen (see Hamalainen ).

variants.name: Hampson
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): patronymic from the Norman personal name Hamo, Hamon (see Hammond ).
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAmhsaigh ‘descendant of Amhsach’ a byname meaning ‘mercenary soldier’ or ‘messenger’, from the adjective amhasach ‘aggressive’.

variants.name: Haimes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Yacov, Yosef.

English: variant, with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s, of Haime, of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Haim (ancient Germanic Haimo, from haim- ‘home’), often Latinized as Hamo. Compare Hammond .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Hammon
meanings: English (Middlesex and Ken): variant of   Hammond  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Hammond was born before 1 April 1574, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Hammond, was 28 and her mother, Rose Trippe, was 21. She married George Kelsey in 1599, in Chelmsford, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 25 November 1631, in Suffolk, England, and was buried in Little Thurlow, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Hammond est née avant 1 avril 1574, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Hammond, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Rose Trippe, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé George Kelsey en 1599, à Chelmsford, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée le 25 novembre 1631, à Suffolk, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Little Thurlow, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Hammond vor 1. April 1574 in Lavenham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Hammond 28 und ihre Mutter Rose Trippe 21. Sie hat 1599 in Chelmsford, Essex, England George Kelsey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb am 25. November 1631 in Suffolk, England und wurde in Little Thurlow, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hammond è nata il prima del 1 aprile 1574 nel Lavenham, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Thomas Hammond, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Rose Trippe, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato George Kelsey nel 1599, nel Chelmsford, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 25 novembre 1631, nel Suffolk, England, e fu sepolta a Little Thurlow, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hammondが1574年4月1日以前にイギリスイギリススフォルクラベンハムで生まれた時，父Thomas Hammondは28歳，母Rose Trippeは21歳であった。1599年にイギリスエセックスチェルムスフォードでGeorge Kelseyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1631年11月25日にイギリススフォルクで死去し，イギリススフォルクリットル スウルロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hammond(이)가 1574년 4월 1일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 레이븐함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Hammond(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Rose Trippe(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1599년 영국 에세스 헬스퍼드에서 George Kelsey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1631년 11월 25일 영국 수폴크에서 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 리틀 스를로에 묻혔다.

pt.before 1 April 1574–25 November 1631: antes 1 de abril de 1574–25 de novembro de 1631
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hammond nasceu antes 1 de abril de 1574, em Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas Hammond, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Rose Trippe, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com George Kelsey em 1599, em Chelmsford, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 25 de novembro de 1631, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Little Thurlow, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Hammond родилась перед 1 апрель 1574 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Hammond было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Rose Trippe, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George Kelsey в 1599 г. в местности челмсфорд, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла 25 ноября 1631 г. в местности суффолк, Англия и была похоронена в местности литл турлоу, суффолк, Англия.
